recalls.Description: Snap has issued a recall for the rechargeable lithium-ion battery that accompanies the Pixy Flying Camera. This compact camera, which captures photos and videos, measures approximately five inches by four inches and is yellow in color. The battery, also yellow, is about three inches long and half an inch wide. There is a significant fire hazard associated with this battery as it can overheat during use.
recalls.Title: Snap Recalls Lithium-Ion Battery for Pixy Flying Cameras Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Snap's recall hotline at 800-269-6990, available 24/7. They can also email support@pixy.com or visit https://support.pixy.com/recall. More information can be found by navigating to www.pixy.com and clicking on the 'Recalls' link at the bottom of the page. Snap is proactively contacting all known purchasers.

Products.Name: Rechargeable Lithium-Ion Battery for Pixy Flying Cameras

Images.Caption: Recalled battery for Pixy Flying Camera
Pixy Flying Camera with recalled battery on charger

Injuries.Name: Snap has received four reports of the battery overheating and bulging, resulting in one minor battery fire and one minor injury.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Pixy.com and Amazon.com from May 2022 to December 2023, priced between $185 and $250 for the Pixy Flying Camera or 'Flight Pack' bundle, $40 to $50 for the extra charger/battery, and $16 to $20 for the battery sold separately.

Importers.Name: Snap LLC., of Santa Monica, California

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential overheating of the lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the Pixy Flying Camera immediately, remove the battery, and cease charging it. They should visit www.pixy.com for guidance on participating in the recall, obtaining a refund, and disposing of the battery safely according to local regulations. Refunds are also available for the Pixy flying camera and other accessories.

recalls.metaDescription: Snap recalls lithium-ion batteries for Pixy Flying Cameras due to fire risks. Learn how to participate in the recall and obtain a refund.
